title: Clean Fuels Weekly Commentary: Type 1 Prices Increase While Volumes Decline WoW- CARB's LCFS Amendments Disapproved by OAL
content:
This Weekâ€™s Highlights:

(10th February â€“ 16th February)
  • A total of 704,135 credits were exchanged as Type 1 trades, a fall of 55.9% week-over-week (WoW), and all non-zero trades witnessed a fall of 46.2% WoW as 1,279,135 credits were traded in CA LCFS.
  • The weekly average CA LCFS credit price increased this week to $74.16/MTÂ for Type 1Â transfers, recording a 1.67%Â rise WoW; All Non-Zero transfers fell by 7.26% in price, reaching $66.06/MT.
  • The average LCFS spot credit price decreased by 1.09% WoW; on 18th Feb, the spot price stood at $72.25/MT and the benchmark price stood at $77.13/MT.
  • D3 RIN prices increased by 1.22% WoW, D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 3.85% WoW.

cCarbonâ€™s Market View

In the week from February 10th to 16th, Type 1 credit volumes saw a notable drop, while prices continued to show some upward movement. Trading volumes fell significantly week-over-week, reflecting a decrease in overall market activity. However, the average price for Type 1 transfers rose slightly, maintaining a positive trend, although other non-zero transfers saw a slight price decrease. Spot prices also dipped a bit, while RIN prices across various categories saw moderate increases. Meanwhile, on February 18th, the California Air Resources Board (CARB) received a Notice of Disapproval from the Office of Administrative Law (OAL) regarding its proposed amendments to the Low Carbon Fuel Standard (LCFS) Regulation. OAL identified inconsistencies in the regulatory language, and CARB plans to address these issues before resubmitting the amendments within the next 120 days. In the meantime, CARB will continue to implement the current LCFS regulation which became effective in July 2020.

CA LCFS Weekly Price and Volume Trend

[caption id="attachment_592385" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 1. Weekly average credit prices and weekly volumes, Source: California ARB[/caption] The average Type 1 prices recorded a 1.67% WoW rise, increasing from $72.94 to $74.16 this week, with all non-zero credit prices decreased by 7.26% WoW, falling from $71.23 to $66.06. Type 1 trades saw 704,135 credits exchanged, reflecting a decline of 55.90% WoW. The total transaction volume for all non-zero trades saw a decrease of 46.20% to reach 1,279,135 credits.

CA LCFS Daily Average Spot Price

[caption id="attachment_592386" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 2. California LCFS Weekly average spot prices and benchmark (*CC.Info OTC: Numbers are an average of broker prices reported to CC.info)[/caption] Last week, the average LCFS spot credit prices decreased by 1.09% WoW, reaching $73.50/MT. On 18th February, the average spot price was $72.25/MT, while the benchmark, representing the future price of contracts expiring at the end of 2025, stood at $77.13/MT on 18th February.

CA LCFS Weekly Trade Frequency and Volume

[caption id="attachment_592387" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 3. Trade frequency and volume, Source: California ARB[/caption] As per the data reported by CARB last week, 32 trades were reported in the market, with eight high-volume trades. The highest transfer by price was $80.00 for a Type 2 transaction at a volume of 5,000 credits. Last week, the lowest transfer by price was $44.70 per credit of Type 2, reported for a transaction at volume of 95,000 credits. The largest volume transfers of 251,000 credits of the Type 1 category were undertaken for a price of $76.57, whereas the smallest transfer volume was reported for a transaction of 473 credits of the Type 1 category at $73.50.

CA LCFS Monthly Volumes and Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_592388" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 4. Total monthly volumes and associated prices, Source: California ARB[/caption] In January, the credit volume totalled 6.72 MMT, slightly down from December's 7.38 MMT. However, the Type 1 average spot price saw a small increase, rising from $71.89 in December 2024 to $72.56 in January 2025. Trading volumes also experienced significant year-over-year growth, up by 31.25% compared to the 5.12 MMT recorded in January of the previous year. By February 16, the Type 1 average spot price had climbed further to $73.31, while volumes stood at 3.66 MMT.

CA LCFS Weekly Volume Price Trend

[caption id="attachment_592389" align="alignnone" width="1000"] Fig 5. Volume Price Trend indicator for the CA LCFS market, Source: California ARB[/caption] The Volume Price Trend Indicator (VPT) helps identify the parity between supply and demand for a stock. The idea behind the indicator is to multiply the market volume of LCFS credits by the percentage change in its price. If the credit price declines, the value of the indicator falls. Across 10th Feb â€“ 16th Feb, total VPT recorded a 19.89% decrease (WoW), with all non-zero prices falling over the previous week.

Renewable Fuel Standard (RFS): RIN weekly price trend

RINs are credits used for compliance and are the â€œcurrencyâ€ of the RFS program. Renewable fuel producers generate RINs, market participants trade RINs, and obligated parties obtain and then ultimately retire RINs for the compliance list. D3 RIN prices increased by 1.22% WoW, D4, D5 and D6 RIN prices increased by 3.85% WoW. D3: Cellulosic biofuel D4: Biomass-based diesel D5: Advanced biofuels (produced from any type of renewable biomass except corn starch ethanol) D6: Conventional renewable biofuel ethanol derived from corn starch, or any other qualifying renewable fuel

Upcoming dates

Key Clean Fuels Market Developments this week

  • Indiana Senate Passes Legislation to Incentivize Higher Ethanol Blends at Gas Stations. The Indiana Senate passed a bill SB 254 on Monday that would provide tax credits to gas stations that sell higher blends of biofuels like ethanol â€” as well as credits for companies that make and blend the fuel. Indiana is one of the top states for ethanol production and more than 40 percent of the stateâ€™s corn crop is used for it.
  • Trump Administration Approves Sustainable Aviation Fuel Refinery Loan. President Donald Trump's energy department said on Tuesday it approved the disbursement of a loan guarantee that had been finalized days before he took office to Calumet (CLMT.O), opens new tab for the expansion of a sustainable aviation fuel refinery in Montana. The Montana Renewables refinery in Great Falls opened in late 2022 and produces about 140 million gallons a year of biofuels. The loan will allow it to expand production to 315 million gallons per year, and produce about half of North American SAF, a fuel made from fats from seed oils and tallow that is lower in greenhouse gas emissions than conventional jet fuel.
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Announces â€œFair and Reciprocal Planâ€ on Trade. President Donald Trump has directed his administration to explore potential retaliation against Brazilâ€™s ethanol trade policies, which impose an 18% tariff on U.S. ethanol exports, compared to the U.S. tariff of just 2.5%. In 2024, this disparity led to the U.S. importing over $200 million in ethanol from Brazil, while only exporting $52 million to Brazil.
